name: Amos Herr Park
description: Amos Herr Park is a place to play pickleball in Salunga-Landisville, PA. There are 4 outdoor hard and acrylic courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and wheelchair accessibility. The courts are free.

name: Bridle Path Park
description: Bridle Path Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Marietta, PA. There is 1 outdoor asphalt and concrete court. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The court is free.

name: Brubaker Park
description: Brubaker Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Denver, PA. There are 8 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like lights. The courts are free.

name: Community Memorial Park
description: Community Memorial Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in New Holland, PA. There are 6 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are free.

name: D.F. Buchmiller Park
description: Come play pickleball at D.F. Buchmiller Park in Lancaster, PA! There are 6 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms. The courts are free. Reservations are not available.
hours: Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 5 p. m. description: Come play pickleball at Elizabeth Township, Lancaster Co. in Lititz, PA! There is 1 outdoor court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. The court is free.

description: Ephrata Recreation Center is a place to play pickleball in Ephrata, PA. There are 6 hard courts. 3 are outdoors and 3 are indoors. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play.

description: Fuhrman Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Maytown, PA. There is 1 outdoor concrete court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. Players have access to restrooms. The court cannot be reserved. The court is free.

name: Lampeter-Strasburg Family YMCA
description: Lampeter-Strasburg Family YMCA is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Lancaster, PA. There are 6 courts. 4 are outdoors and 2 are indoors. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The facility includes amenities like locker rooms, restrooms, trainers or lessons, wheelchair accessibility, adaptive programming, water, and youth programming. A membership is required to play.
fees: Members only — Members free for play, guest passes easily available

name: St. Joseph Catholic Club
description:
Our facility offers two (2) courts on wooden gym floor, air condition in the summer and heat in
the winter. Tape lines have been laid, with portable nets. Amenities include pickleball balls, bottled water, restrooms and FREE off-street parking. 

Rental paddles available.
All skill levels are welcomed. Open play is available. Conveniently located just 3 blocks from the square in downtown Lancaster.
open_play_schedule: Call Denny at (717) 945-2170 for scheduling.  We will be closed on:  Oct. 29th , Nov. 5th, Dec. 24th, 26th, and 31.

name: Warehouse 100
description: Includes one hour of rental time in our climate controlled venue. Our Pickleball court is completley private, and you get the space to yourself. Clients are allowed to bring your own music, snacks, and beverages. Water fountains and restrooms are also available at the venue.
open_play_schedule: Players may click the link and view available time slots.
